title: Vlog 8-18 DUI for weed trial 10-10. Representing myself. I think I'm Autistic

enrichment.summary: Kyle updates his followers on his DUI-for-marijuana case, describing a pre-trial hearing where he chose to represent himself, a self-conducted sobriety test experiment while heavily stoned to document his tolerance, and his plan to argue habitual marijuana use and a suspected Asperger's diagnosis as part of his defense at a September 10th jury trial.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: The 5-nanogram legal limit is arbitrary and doesn't account for habituated users who function normally at higher blood levels
Marijuana helps him manage Asperger's symptoms — calms him down, extends focus, and makes him smarter/more productive
Prefers to represent himself so he can speak in court directly, inspired by Erin Brockovich
Weed improves his balance and athletic performance, not impairs it — doesn't make sense it would cause him to fail a sobriety test
Habitual users should get a 'marijuana sponsorship' on their license to exempt them from impairment thresholds
Believes he has Asperger's based on his mother's observations and his own traits — obsessive focus, list-making, social awkwardness, insomnia since birth

entities: Indiana dispensary worker (acquitted DUI case referenced)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been working on his website and plans to update it more frequently.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle received a $10 donation on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle attended a pre-trial hearing for his case on the last Thursday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle went to the hearing with his mom at 8:30 AM in Denver.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle decided to represent himself in court after being inspired by the movie 'Erin Brockovich.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's trial is scheduled for September 10th at 12:30 PM with a jury of 12.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle mentioned a case of a 29-year-old girl from Indiana who was acquitted after being arrested for driving with marijuana in her system.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes he needs to stay high all the time to avoid going crazy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle told the prosecutor about a 5-year-old video where he explained that his left leg shakes uncontrollably when he gets nervous.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle deleted the video because he found it embarrassing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle mentioned that he failed to communicate with his roommate about his insurance and registration.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle took a herhological test and scored 7.1, which is seven times the threshold for failing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's blood test showed a THC level of 9.5 nanograms, almost double the legal limit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes he was not actually stoned when driving because he is habituated to marijuana.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle smoked hash for several hours before taking a drug test.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle took the same drug test as the one administered the night of his arrest and expects the results to be higher.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle performed balance tests after smoking marijuana and felt his balance was better.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes smoking marijuana helps him relax and work more efficiently.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's mom thinks he has Asperger's syndrome because of his intense focus on his website and temper tantrums.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was born on January 7th, 1974, after his mother was induced with oxytocin.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's mom believes his inability to sleep through the night as a baby was due to the oxytocin.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has no memories from before the age of four.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes marijuana enhances the traits associated with Asperger's syndrome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels marijuana makes him smarter and more creative.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to get a medical marijuana license for his defense.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes marijuana sponsorship on licenses could solve issues with driving under the influence of marijuana.
